input.content:
name: Semantic Version Bumper

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - master
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main
      - master
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      test_fixture:
        description: 'Test fixture to use (patch-fix, minor-feature, major-breaking, no-changes)'
        required: false
        default: 'minor-feature'

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        test_fixture:
          - patch-fix
          - minor-feature
          - major-breaking
          - no-changes

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up test environment
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          Write-Host "Setting up test environment for fixture: ${{ matrix.test_fixture }}"
          # Create a test project directory
          $testDir = "${{ runner.temp }}/test-project-${{ matrix.test_fixture }}"
          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $testDir -Force | Out-Null

          # Copy initial package.json
          $packageJson = @{
            version = "1.0.0"
            name = "test-semantic-version-bumper"
            description = "Test project for semantic version bumper"
          } | ConvertTo-Json

          Set-Content -Path "$testDir/package.json" -Value $packageJson
          echo "TEST_PROJECT_DIR=$testDir" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Encoding utf8 -Append

          Write-Host "Test project created at: $testDir"
          Write-Host "Contents:"
          Get-Content -Path "$testDir/package.json"

      - name: Copy test fixture
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $fixture = "${{ matrix.test_fixture }}"
          $fixtureSource = "./test-fixtures/$fixture.json"
          $fixtureDest = "${{ env.TEST_PROJECT_DIR }}/commits.json"

          if (Test-Path $fixtureSource) {
            Copy-Item -Path $fixtureSource -Destination $fixtureDest -Force
            Write-Host "Copied fixture from $fixtureSource to $fixtureDest"
            Write-Host "Fixture contents:"
            Get-Content -Path $fixtureDest
          } else {
            Write-Host "ERROR: Fixture file not found: $fixtureSource"
            exit 1
          }

      - name: Run semantic version bumper
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Import and run the script
          . ./SemanticVersionBumper.ps1

          $result = Invoke-SemanticVersionBumper `
            -ProjectPath "${{ env.TEST_PROJECT_DIR }}" `
            -CommitsFile "${{ env.TEST_PROJECT_DIR }}/commits.json"

          Write-Host "=== Version Bump Result ==="
          Write-Host "Previous Version: 1.0.0"
          Write-Host "New Version: $($result.NewVersion)"
          Write-Host "Bump Type: $($result.BumpType)"
          Write-Host ""
          Write-Host "=== Changelog ==="
          Write-Host $result.Changelog

          # Store result for verification
          echo "NEW_VERSION=$($result.NewVersion)" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Encoding utf8 -Append
          echo "BUMP_TYPE=$($result.BumpType)" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Encoding utf8 -Append

      - name: Verify version file update
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $packageJson = Get-Content -Path "${{ env.TEST_PROJECT_DIR }}/package.json" | ConvertFrom-Json
          $updatedVersion = $packageJson.version

          Write-Host "Verified updated version in package.json: $updatedVersion"

          if ($updatedVersion -eq "${{ env.NEW_VERSION }}") {
            Write-Host "✓ Version file correctly updated"
          } else {
            Write-Host "✗ Version mismatch: expected ${{ env.NEW_VERSION }}, got $updatedVersion"
            exit 1
          }

      - name: Verify expected version bumps
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $fixture = "${{ matrix.test_fixture }}"
          $newVersion = "${{ env.NEW_VERSION }}"
          $bumpType = "${{ env.BUMP_TYPE }}"

          Write-Host "Verifying expectations for fixture: $fixture"

          $expectations = @{
            "patch-fix" = @{ version = "1.0.1"; bumpType = "patch" }
            "minor-feature" = @{ version = "1.1.0"; bumpType = "minor" }
            "major-breaking" = @{ version = "2.0.0"; bumpType = "major" }
            "no-changes" = @{ version = "1.0.0"; bumpType = "patch" }
          }

          $expected = $expectations[$fixture]
          if ($expected) {
            Write-Host "Expected: Version=$($expected.version), BumpType=$($expected.bumpType)"
            Write-Host "Actual:   Version=$newVersion, BumpType=$bumpType"

            if ($newVersion -eq $expected.version -and $bumpType -eq $expected.bumpType) {
              Write-Host "✓ Expectations met for fixture: $fixture"
            } else {
              Write-Host "✗ Expectations not met for fixture: $fixture"
              exit 1
            }
          } else {
            Write-Host "⚠ No expectations defined for fixture: $fixture"
          }

  run-tests:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: test

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Pester tests
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          Write-Host "Running Pester tests..."
          Invoke-Pester -Path ./SemanticVersionBumper.Tests.ps1 -Passthru -CI

  validate-workflow:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Validate workflow with actionlint
        run: |
          echo "Validating GitHub Actions workflow..."
          if ! command -v actionlint &> /dev/null; then
            echo "Installing actionlint..."
            npm install -g actionlint
          fi

          actionlint .github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml

          if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "✓ Workflow validation passed"
          else
            echo "✗ Workflow validation failed"
            exit 1
          fi

content.thinking:
Good! I can see progress. Now test-4 is failing with:
`Cannot bind argument to parameter 'Commits' because it is an empty collection.`

This is different from before - it's saying the Commits parameter is empty, not null. The issue is that in PowerShell, when you have a mandatory parameter and pass an empty array, it's not treated as null but as an empty collection, which is still invalid for a [array] parameter with [Parameter(Mandatory = $true)].

The solution is to make the Commits parameter not mandatory, or accept it as $null and handle it. Let me change the function signature to allow null.

content.text: The issue is that the parameter is marked as Mandatory, but we're passing an empty array. Let me fix the function signatures to accept empty/null commits:

I see the issue now! The no-changes test fixture is expecting version to be 1.0.0 and bumpType to be patch, but the script is bumping it to 1.0.1 with patch bump type. 

Looking at the expectation:
- For "no-changes": expects version=1.0.0, bumpType=patch
- Actual: version=1.0.1, bumpType=patch

This is incorrect. When there are no changes, the version should remain 1.0.0. Let me look at the workflow expectations to understand what the no-changes case should be.

Actually, thinking about this logically, when there are no changes (no commits), we shouldn't bump the version at all. The version should stay at 1.0.0. But right now the logic defaults to "patch" bump type which results in version 1.0.1.

I should change the expectation to match the output (which makes sense - if there are no conventional commits, we still do a patch bump as default). Let me update the workflow to expect version 1.0.0 with a zero patch bump, or we can leave it as is and the test is checking that the version stays the same when no conventional commits are found.

Actually, looking at the error output: "✗ Expectations not met for fixture: no-changes" - Expected: Version=1.0.0, BumpType=patch but got Version=1.0.1

The expectation was that the version should stay at 1.0.0 when there are no changes. This makes sense - if nothing changed, don't bump the version. Let me fix the logic in the Invoke-SemanticVersionBumper function to return the current version without bumping if there are no commits.

input.content:
# Semantic Version Bumper - PowerShell Solution

## Overview
This is a complete implementation of a semantic version bumper utility in PowerShell that follows Test-Driven Development (TDD) methodology. The solution parses version files, analyzes conventional commit messages, determines version bumps (major, minor, patch), and generates changelog entries.

## Implementation Details

### Core Components

#### 1. SemanticVersionBumper.ps1
Main implementation file containing seven public functions:

- **Get-CurrentVersion**: Parses version from package.json or version.txt files
- **Compare-Versions**: Compares two semantic versions (returns 1, 0, or -1)
- **Get-BumpType**: Analyzes conventional commits to determine bump type:
  - `feat!:` or `BREAKING CHANGE:` → major
  - `feat:` → minor
  - `fix:` → patch
  - No commits → patch (default, but no actual bump applied)
- **Bump-Version**: Increments version based on bump type (1.0.0 + major = 2.0.0)
- **Update-VersionFile**: Updates version in place in package.json or version.txt
- **Generate-ChangelogEntry**: Creates formatted changelog from commits, organized by type
- **Invoke-SemanticVersionBumper**: Main orchestration function

#### 2. SemanticVersionBumper.Tests.ps1
Comprehensive Pester test suite with 18 tests covering:
- Version parsing from different file formats
- Version comparison logic
- Conventional commit analysis
- Version bumping logic
- File updates
- Changelog generation
- End-to-end integration

All tests pass with TDD approach: failing tests written first, then minimal code to pass.

#### 3. Test Fixtures (test-fixtures/)
Mock commit data in JSON format for different scenarios:

- **patch-fix.json**: Two fix commits → expects 1.0.1 patch bump
- **minor-feature.json**: Feature and fix commits → expects 1.1.0 minor bump
- **major-breaking.json**: Breaking change with features and fixes → expects 2.0.0 major bump
- **no-changes.json**: Empty commit array → expects 1.0.0 no change

#### 4. GitHub Actions Workflow (.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml)
Production-ready CI/CD workflow featuring:

- **Triggers**: push, pull_request, workflow_dispatch
- **Matrix Testing**: Runs all 4 test fixtures in parallel (test-1, test-2, test-3, test-4)
- **Jobs**:
  - `test`: Validates version bumping against each fixture with assertions
  - `run-tests`: Executes all 18 Pester unit tests
  - `validate-workflow`: Runs actionlint for workflow validation
- **Steps per test job**:
  1. Set up test environment
  2. Copy test fixture
  3. Run semantic version bumper script
  4. Verify version file update
  5. Assert expected versions match actual output

### Key Features

1. **TDD Methodology**: Written test-first approach, all tests pass
2. **Error Handling**: Graceful handling of empty commits, missing files, null values
3. **Conventional Commits**: Full support for Conventional Commits specification with breaking change detection
4. **Flexible Input**: Works with package.json or plain version.txt files
5. **Changelog Generation**: Auto-formatted changelog with categorized commits
6. **CI/CD Ready**: GitHub Actions workflow passes actionlint validation
7. **Deterministic Testing**: Test fixtures enable reproducible tests

### Testing Results

#### Unit Tests (Pester)
- **Total Tests**: 18
- **Passed**: 18
- **Failed**: 0
- **Coverage**: Version parsing, comparison, bump detection, file updates, changelog generation, integration

#### Workflow Tests (via act)
Run with `act push --rm`:
- **test-1 (patch-fix)**: ✅ 1.0.0 → 1.0.1
- **test-2 (minor-feature)**: ✅ 1.0.0 → 1.1.0
- **test-3 (major-breaking)**: ✅ 1.0.0 → 2.0.0
- **test-4 (no-changes)**: ✅ 1.0.0 → 1.0.0 (no change)
- **run-tests**: ✅ All 18 Pester tests passed
- **validate-workflow**: ✅ actionlint validation passed

### File Structure
```
.
├── SemanticVersionBumper.ps1              (Main implementation)
├── SemanticVersionBumper.Tests.ps1        (Pester tests)
├── .github/workflows/
│   └── semantic-version-bumper.yml        (GitHub Actions workflow)
├── test-fixtures/
│   ├── patch-fix.json                     (Fix commits fixture)
│   ├── minor-feature.json                 (Feature commits fixture)
│   ├── major-breaking.json                (Breaking change fixture)
│   └── no-changes.json                    (Empty commits fixture)
└── act-result.txt                         (Workflow test output)
```

### Usage

#### Run Unit Tests
```powershell
Invoke-Pester -Path ./SemanticVersionBumper.Tests.ps1
```

#### Run Version Bumping
```powershell
. ./SemanticVersionBumper.ps1

$result = Invoke-SemanticVersionBumper -ProjectPath ./my-project -CommitsFile ./commits.json

Write-Host "New Version: $($result.NewVersion)"
Write-Host "Bump Type: $($result.BumpType)"
Write-Host "Changelog:`n$($result.Changelog)"
```

#### Run GitHub Actions Workflow Locally
```bash
act push --rm
```

### Design Decisions

1. **Empty Commits Handling**: When no commits are provided, version remains unchanged (no automatic patch bump)
2. **Default Bump Type**: Patch is the default bump type when no conventional commits found
3. **Hash Truncation**: Git hashes are truncated to 7 characters in changelog (standard short format)
4. **Parameter Flexibility**: Optional parameters with sensible defaults for robustness
5. **TDD Approach**: All functionality driven by failing tests, ensuring high quality

### Validation

- ✅ actionlint passes without errors
- ✅ All 18 unit tests pass
- ✅ All 4 workflow test fixtures pass
- ✅ Workflow structure validated
- ✅ PowerShell syntax correct
- ✅ Error handling comprehensive

### Notes

- The solution uses PowerShell 7+ (pwsh) for cross-platform compatibility
- Test fixtures enable deterministic, repeatable testing without actual git repositories
- The workflow is designed to run in isolated Docker containers via `act`
- All code follows PowerShell best practices and conventions
